S.FORM: 'akapito toki omoalrao gako.
S.PHON: ʔakapito toki omoaɭao gako.
S.FORM: 'akapito toki omoalrao gako.
S.PHON: ʔakapito toki omoaɭao gako.
S.TRANSL: 我今天七點到學校。
I arrived at school at seven today.

S.FORM: osolatenga, sinsi modha'ane taikikiyae solate, caha'enai vanidho mani iya 'ina, "idhi'iya! sokova!"
S.PHON: osolatəŋa, sinsi moðaʔanə taikikijaə solatə, ʦahaʔənai vaniðo mani ija ʔina, "iðiʔija! sokova!"
S.FORM: osolatenga, sinsi modha'ane taikikiyae solate, caha'enai vanidho mani iya 'ina, "idhi'iya! sokova!"
S.PHON: osolatəŋa, sinsi moðaʔanə taikikijaə solatə, ʦahaʔənai vaniðo mani ija ʔina, "iðiʔija! sokova!"
S.TRANSL: 上課了，老師走進教室，班長說:＂起立!敬禮!＂
It's time for class , the teacher walked into the classroom, and the monitor said: "Stand up! Salute!"

S.FORM: cekelaenai poke'ecengae miya 'ina, "sinsi aika'opolringao."
S.PHON: ʦəkəlaənai pokəʔəʦəŋaə mija ʔina, "sinsi aikaʔopoɭiŋao."
S.FORM: cekelaenai poke'ecengae miya 'ina, "sinsi aika'opolringao."
S.PHON: ʦəkəlaənai pokəʔəʦəŋaə mija ʔina, "sinsi aikaʔopoɭiŋao."
S.TRANSL: 我們大聲的說:＂老師好。＂
We said loudly, "Hi, teacher."

S.FORM: osolatengani, singisi mani paoto'oto'iname 'aa'ia'ipi kaocae vaha.
S.PHON: osolatəŋani, siŋisi mani paotoʔotoʔinamə ʔaaʔiaʔipi kaoʦaə vaha.
S.FORM: osolatengani, singisi mani paoto'oto'iname 'aa'ia'ipi kaocae vaha.
S.PHON: osolatəŋani, siŋisi mani paotoʔotoʔinamə ʔaaʔiaʔipi kaoʦaə vaha.
S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，老師教我們數字的族語。
In class, the teacher taught us the Rukai language of numbers.

S.FORM: mani 'anopapalranai sinsi o'ipi, omale 'a'iya'ipi omale.
S.PHON: mani ʔanopapaɭanai sinsi oʔipi, omalə ʔaʔijaʔipi omalə.
S.FORM: mani 'anopapalranai sinsi o'ipi, omale 'a'iya'ipi omale.
S.PHON: mani ʔanopapaɭanai sinsi oʔipi, omalə ʔaʔijaʔipi omalə.
S.TRANSL: 我們跟著老師唸，我們唱了數字歌。
We followed the teacher to read, we sang the number song.

S.FORM: 'ica'oca'onai ta kadhao kaocae vaha.
S.PHON: ʔiʦaʔoʦaʔonai ta kaðao kaoʦaə vaha.
S.FORM: 'ica'oca'onai ta kadhao kaocae vaha.
S.PHON: ʔiʦaʔoʦaʔonai ta kaðao kaoʦaə vaha.
S.TRANSL: 我學會好多族語。
I have learned many Rukai languages.

S.FORM: pamenanaelrao moa gako, pamenanaelrao 'ica'oca'o kaocae vaha, amo 'aovaovahalrao pasiyalalalra inali.
S.PHON: pamənanaəɭao moa gako, pamənanaəɭao ʔiʦaʔoʦaʔo kaoʦaə vaha, amo ʔaovaovahaɭao pasijalalaɭa inali.
S.FORM: pamenanaelrao moa gako, pamenanaelrao 'ica'oca'o kaocae vaha, amo 'aovaovahalrao pasiyalalalra inali.
S.PHON: pamənanaəɭao moa gako, pamənanaəɭao ʔiʦaʔoʦaʔo kaoʦaə vaha, amo ʔaovaovahaɭao pasijalalaɭa inali.
S.TRANSL: 我天天上學，我天天學族語，我要講給媽媽聽。
I go to school every day, I learn the Rukai language every day, and I want to tell my mother.

S.FORM: alraceli 'a Vaaho omiiyae.
S.PHON: aɭaʦəli ʔa vaaho omiijaə.
S.FORM: alraceli 'a Vaaho omiiyae.
S.PHON: aɭaʦəli ʔa vaaho omiijaə.
S.TRANSL: 我的名字是Vaaho。
My name is Vaaho.

S.FORM: amali alraceni 'a Hiyao.
S.PHON: amali aɭaʦəni ʔa hijao.
S.FORM: amali alraceni 'a Hiyao.
S.PHON: amali aɭaʦəni ʔa hijao.
S.TRANSL: 我爸爸的名字是Hiyao。
My dad's name is Hiyao.

S.FORM: inali alraceni 'a Leeve.
S.PHON: inali aɭaʦəni ʔa ləəvə.
S.FORM: inali alraceni 'a Leeve.
S.PHON: inali aɭaʦəni ʔa ləəvə.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽的名字是Leeve。
My mother's name is Leeve.

S.FORM: mene caili otahamangelelrao caili.
S.PHON: mənə ʦaili otahamaŋələɭao ʦaili.
S.FORM: mene caili otahamangelelrao caili.
S.PHON: mənə ʦaili otahamaŋələɭao ʦaili.
S.TRANSL: 我今年10歲。
I am 10 years old.

S.FORM: omikiyae ta kaeaea taka 'aolai, alracedha 'a taotao, mene caili otahamangele la kaeaea caili.
S.PHON: omikijaə ta kaəaəa taka ʔaolai, aɭaʦəða ʔa taotao, mənə ʦaili otahamaŋələ la kaəaəa ʦaili.
S.FORM: omikiyae ta kaeaea taka 'aolai, alracedha 'a taotao, mene caili otahamangele la kaeaea caili.
S.PHON: omikijaə ta kaəaəa taka ʔaolai, aɭaʦəða ʔa taotao, mənə ʦaili otahamaŋələ la kaəaəa ʦaili.
S.TRANSL: 我有一個哥哥，他叫taotao，他今年11歲。
I have a brother, his name is taotao, he is 11 years old.

S.FORM: ta'aelrenai miki ta kaeaea gako solasolate.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭənai miki ta kaəaəa gako solasolatə.
S.FORM: ta'aelrenai miki ta kaeaea gako solasolate.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭənai miki ta kaəaəa gako solasolatə.
S.TRANSL: 我們在同一間學校讀書。
We study in the same school.

S.FORM: ilraae la takali 'aolai madhalamenai omale.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la takali ʔaolai maðalamənai omalə.
S.FORM: ilraae la takali 'aolai madhalamenai omale.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la takali ʔaolai maðalamənai omalə.
S.TRANSL: 我和哥哥都喜歡唱歌。
Both my brother and I like to sing.

S.FORM: madhalamelrao longolangoi, lo taivalrivalriyae, cekelaenai dha'ananae moa longolangoi.
S.PHON: maðalaməɭao loŋolaŋoi, lo taivaɭivaɭijaə, ʦəkəlaənai ðaʔananaə moa loŋolaŋoi.
S.FORM: madhalamelrao longolangoi, lo taivalrivalriyae, cekelaenai dha'ananae moa longolangoi.
S.PHON: maðalaməɭao loŋolaŋoi, lo taivaɭivaɭijaə, ʦəkəlaənai ðaʔananaə moa loŋolaŋoi.
S.TRANSL: 我喜歡游泳，放假的時候，我們全家會一起去游泳。
I like swimming. During the holidays, our whole family go swimming together.

S.FORM: madhalamelrao tova'oae la'ivoko, odholromao ta'aelre omale la longolangoi.
S.PHON: maðalaməɭao tovaʔoaə laʔivoko, oðoɭomao taʔaəɭə omalə la loŋolaŋoi.
S.FORM: madhalamelrao tova'oae la'ivoko, odholromao ta'aelre omale la longolangoi.
S.PHON: maðalaməɭao tovaʔoaə laʔivoko, oðoɭomao taʔaəɭə omalə la loŋolaŋoi.
S.TRANSL: 我喜歡認識新朋友，我們可以一起唱歌和游泳。
I love meeting new people, we can sing and swim together.

S.FORM: ilraae 'a oponoholrao lroolai.
S.PHON: iɭaaə ʔa oponohoɭao ɭoolai.
S.FORM: ilraae 'a oponoholrao lroolai.
S.PHON: iɭaaə ʔa oponohoɭao ɭoolai.
S.TRANSL: 我是萬山的小孩。
I am an oponoholrao child.

S.FORM: dha'anenai omiki ka'oponohae.
S.PHON: ðaʔanənai omiki kaʔoponohaə.
S.FORM: dha'anenai omiki ka'oponohae.
S.PHON: ðaʔanənai omiki kaʔoponohaə.
S.TRANSL: 我們家住在舊萬山。
Our family lives in ka'oponohae.

S.FORM: ta'alrimanai ocao dha'ananae.
S.PHON: taʔaɭimanai oʦao ðaʔananaə.
S.FORM: ta'alrimanai ocao dha'ananae.
S.PHON: taʔaɭimanai oʦao ðaʔananaə.
S.TRANSL: 我們家共有五個人。
There are five people in our family.

S.FORM: dha'ananaenai omiki ta katolro pa'atakatakali 'aolai la 'avai.
S.PHON: ðaʔananaənai omiki ta katoɭo paʔatakatakali ʔaolai la ʔavai.
S.FORM: dha'ananaenai omiki ta katolro pa'atakatakali 'aolai la 'avai.
S.PHON: ðaʔananaənai omiki ta katoɭo paʔatakatakali ʔaolai la ʔavai.
S.TRANSL: 我們家有三個兄弟姊妹。
There are three brothers and sisters in our family.

S.FORM: ta'aelrelrao amali, inali, takali 'aolai la takali 'avai.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭəɭao amali, inali, takali ʔaolai la takali ʔavai.
S.FORM: ta'aelrelrao amali, inali, takali 'aolai la takali 'avai.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭəɭao amali, inali, takali ʔaolai la takali ʔavai.
S.TRANSL: 我跟爸爸、媽媽、哥哥和姊姊住在一起。
I live with my father, mother, brother and sister.

S.FORM: amali maka'anahalre, amali 'a kiisace.
S.PHON: amali makaʔanahaɭə, amali ʔa kiisaʦə.
S.FORM: amali maka'anahalre, amali 'a kiisace.
S.PHON: amali makaʔanahaɭə, amali ʔa kiisaʦə.
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸很高，我的爸爸是警察。
My father is tall and he is a policeman.

S.FORM: inali mahilai, inali 'a koisange.
S.PHON: inali mahilai, inali ʔa koisaŋə.
S.FORM: inali mahilai, inali 'a koisange.
S.PHON: inali mahilai, inali ʔa koisaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 我的媽媽很瘦，我的媽媽是醫生。
My mother is thin and my mom is a doctor.

S.FORM: amali omikini dha'ane 'ako'oponoponohainame 'aovaovaha.
S.PHON: amali omikini ðaʔanə ʔakoʔoponoponohainamə ʔaovaovaha.
S.FORM: amali omikini dha'ane 'ako'oponoponohainame 'aovaovaha.
S.PHON: amali omikini ðaʔanə ʔakoʔoponoponohainamə ʔaovaovaha.
S.TRANSL: 爸爸在家會和我們說族語。
My father speaks the Rukai language with us at home.

S.FORM: lo kalalrapa'ae taivalrivalriyae movalriyonai.
S.PHON: lo kalaɭapaʔaə taivaɭivaɭijaə movaɭijonai.
S.FORM: lo kalalrapa'ae taivalrivalriyae movalriyonai.
S.PHON: lo kalaɭapaʔaə taivaɭivaɭijaə movaɭijonai.
S.TRANSL: 我們暑假都會回部落。
We will all go back to the tribe during the summer vacation.

S.FORM: ilraae la latakali 'inyaelrenai moa 'ikohapahapange.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la latakali ʔinjaəɭənai moa ʔikohapahapaŋə.
S.FORM: ilraae la latakali 'inyaelrenai moa 'ikohapahapange.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la latakali ʔinjaəɭənai moa ʔikohapahapaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 我和兄姊會一起抓青蛙。
My brothers and sisters and I will catch frogs together.

S.FORM: madhaalamelrao dha'ananaeli.
S.PHON: maðaalaməɭao ðaʔananaəli.
S.FORM: madhaalamelrao dha'ananaeli.
S.PHON: maðaalaməɭao ðaʔananaəli.
S.TRANSL: 我很喜歡我們家的人。
I like our family very much.

S.FORM: mene ilraae la inali omoanai 'oliliyo.
S.PHON: mənə iɭaaə la inali omoanai ʔolilijo.
S.FORM: mene ilraae la inali omoanai 'oliliyo.
S.PHON: mənə iɭaaə la inali omoanai ʔolilijo.
S.TRANSL: 今天我和媽媽出去玩。
I went out with my mother today.

S.FORM: alidhaa'e tevelengae 'ako kadhalremedheme, kikakeecelre la kikalraapa'a, ve'eve'e maateleke.
S.PHON: aliðaaʔə təvələŋaə ʔako kaðaɭəməðəmə, kikakəəʦəɭə la kikaɭaapaʔa, vəʔəvəʔə maatələkə.
S.FORM: alidhaa'e tevelengae 'ako kadhalremedheme, kikakeecelre la kikalraapa'a, ve'eve'e maateleke.
S.PHON: aliðaaʔə təvələŋaə ʔako kaðaɭəməðəmə, kikakəəʦəɭə la kikaɭaapaʔa, vəʔəvəʔə maatələkə.
S.TRANSL: 白天的天空很藍，天氣不冷也不熱，風很涼爽。
The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

S.FORM: omikinai 'olra'olraae kone minpao, koli'iname votolro'onai, tohamoho ka malraveve.
S.PHON: omikinai ʔoɭaʔoɭaaə konə minpao, koliʔinamə votoɭoʔonai, tohamoho ka maɭavəvə.
S.FORM: omikinai 'olra'olraae kone minpao, koli'iname votolro'onai, tohamoho ka malraveve.
S.PHON: omikinai ʔoɭaʔoɭaaə konə minpao, koliʔinamə votoɭoʔonai, tohamoho ka maɭavəvə.
S.TRANSL: 我們在草地上吃著麵包，陽光灑在身上，非常溫暖。
We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

S.FORM: 'aanenga, tevelengae mani kadhiliangenga.
S.PHON: ʔaanəŋa, təvələŋaə mani kaðiliaŋəŋa.
S.FORM: 'aanenga, tevelengae mani kadhiliangenga.
S.PHON: ʔaanəŋa, təvələŋaə mani kaðiliaŋəŋa.
S.TRANSL: 下午，天空變紅了。
In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

S.FORM: 'itovolrolrao inali, kani kadhiliyangeni tevelengae.
S.PHON: ʔitovoɭoɭao inali, kani kaðilijaŋəni təvələŋaə.
S.FORM: 'itovolrolrao inali, kani kadhiliyangeni tevelengae.
S.PHON: ʔitovoɭoɭao inali, kani kaðilijaŋəni təvələŋaə.
S.TRANSL: 我問媽媽，為甚麼天空是紅色的。
I asked my mother why the sky is red.

S.FORM: inali mani pato'iyae, maelretamo kaava'inga ovelre.
S.PHON: inali mani patoʔijaə, maəɭətamo kaavaʔiŋa ovəɭə.
S.FORM: inali mani pato'iyae, maelretamo kaava'inga ovelre.
S.PHON: inali mani patoʔijaə, maəɭətamo kaavaʔiŋa ovəɭə.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽說，可能是颱風要來了。
Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

S.FORM: maongo tevelengae ikaodho ka keme'e, mokavolenga tahiyo.
S.PHON: maoŋo təvələŋaə ikaoðo ka kəməʔə, mokavoləŋa tahijo.
S.FORM: maongo tevelengae ikaodho ka keme'e, mokavolenga tahiyo.
S.PHON: maoŋo təvələŋaə ikaoðo ka kəməʔə, mokavoləŋa tahijo.
S.TRANSL: 晚上天上沒有雲，星星出來了。
There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

S.FORM: amo oilringanai dha'ane.
S.PHON: amo oiɭiŋanai ðaʔanə.
S.FORM: amo oilringanai dha'ane.
S.PHON: amo oiɭiŋanai ðaʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 我們也要回家了。
We were going home too.

S.FORM: a'oilrinai tavalrevalre'anae, mani 'itovilaro tahiyo.
S.PHON: aʔoiɭinai tavaɭəvaɭəʔanaə, mani ʔitovilaro tahijo.
S.FORM: a'oilrinai tavalrevalre'anae, mani 'itovilaro tahiyo.
S.PHON: aʔoiɭinai tavaɭəvaɭəʔanaə, mani ʔitovilaro tahijo.
S.TRANSL: 在回家的路上，我跟星星許願。
On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

S.FORM: 'amalraine lani kikaava'i ovelre, 'api ooangiae 'oliliyo omiyalrao.
S.PHON: ʔamaɭainə lani kikaavaʔi ovəɭə, ʔapi ooaŋiaə ʔolilijo omijaɭao.
S.FORM: 'amalraine lani kikaava'i ovelre, 'api ooangiae 'oliliyo omiyalrao.
S.PHON: ʔamaɭainə lani kikaavaʔi ovəɭə, ʔapi ooaŋiaə ʔolilijo omijaɭao.
S.TRANSL: 希望颱風不要來，我還想再出去玩。
I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.

S.FORM: gakoli omiki vila'ani dha'anenai, pamenanaelrao dhoadhaace moa gako, la dhadhaacelrao 'oilri.
S.PHON: gakoli omiki vilaʔani ðaʔanənai, pamənanaəɭao ðoaðaaʦə moa gako, la ðaðaaʦəɭao ʔoiɭi.
S.FORM: gakoli omiki vila'ani dha'anenai, pamenanaelrao dhoadhaace moa gako, la dhadhaacelrao 'oilri.
S.PHON: gakoli omiki vilaʔani ðaʔanənai, pamənanaəɭao ðoaðaaʦə moa gako, la ðaðaaʦəɭao ʔoiɭi.
S.TRANSL: 我的學校在我們家旁邊，我天天走路去學校，也走路回家。
My school is next to our house, I walk to school every day, and I also walk home.

S.FORM: gakonai ka matoalraikai, madhaokai ka vanidho, malrihilriho'onai.
S.PHON: gakonai ka matoaɭaikai, maðaokai ka vaniðo, maɭihiɭihoʔonai.
S.FORM: gakonai ka matoalraikai, madhaokai ka vanidho, malrihilriho'onai.
S.PHON: gakonai ka matoaɭaikai, maðaokai ka vaniðo, maɭihiɭihoʔonai.
S.TRANSL: 我們學校不大，沒有很多學生，大家互相認識。
Our school is not big, there are not many students, everyone knows each other.

S.FORM: kapa aolrolainga 'asahovo miki ta'ondhoondhoe', ta'aelre 'osahiyo malri, ta'aelre 'avaevaehe.
S.PHON: kapa aoɭolaiŋa ʔasahovo miki taʔonðoonðoəʔ, taʔaəɭə ʔosahijo maɭi, taʔaəɭə ʔavaəvaəhə.
S.FORM: kapa aolrolainga 'asahovo miki ta'ondhoondhoe', ta'aelre 'osahiyo malri, ta'aelre 'avaevaehe.
S.PHON: kapa aoɭolaiŋa ʔasahovo miki taʔonðoonðoəʔ, taʔaəɭə ʔosahijo maɭi, taʔaəɭə ʔavaəvaəhə.
S.TRANSL: 所有小朋友會聚在操場，一起玩球、一起聊天。
All the children will gather in the playground, play ball and chat together.

S.FORM: sinsinai la ta'aelriname 'osahiyo, gakonai 'a toalrai dha'ananae.
S.PHON: sinsinai la taʔaəɭinamə ʔosahijo, gakonai ʔa toaɭai ðaʔananaə.
S.FORM: sinsinai la ta'aelriname 'osahiyo, gakonai 'a toalrai dha'ananae.
S.PHON: sinsinai la taʔaəɭinamə ʔosahijo, gakonai ʔa toaɭai ðaʔananaə.
S.TRANSL: 老師們也會和我們一起玩，學校是個大家庭。
The teachers also play with us, the school is one big family.

S.FORM: pamenanaenai 'oa'oape, maesalenai sinakenakete taikienai solate.
S.PHON: pamənanaənai ʔoaʔoapə, maəsalənai sinakənakətə taikiənai solatə.
S.FORM: pamenanaenai 'oa'oape, maesalenai sinakenakete taikienai solate.
S.PHON: pamənanaənai ʔoaʔoapə, maəsalənai sinakənakətə taikiənai solatə.
S.TRANSL: 我們每天都要打掃，大家要把教室打掃乾淨。
We have to clean every day, and everyone has to clean the classroom.

S.FORM: omiki ta 'odho'odho ta'avangae, iiki ta 'oa'oape vekenelre.

Some people clean the windows, some sweep the floor.

Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

S.FORM: mo'adhailinganai kipaacengele, 'iidhedhemelraidhe.

S.TRANSL: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
We haven't seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

S.TRANSL: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

S.FORM: omoli 'aolai lredhekeni siikoa malimeme, mataadhi'i kone.

The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

S.FORM: madhalaamelrao oomoli 'aolai 'i 'ociyani, mataadhi'i 'ongolo.

S.TRANSL: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
I also like grandpa's tea, it's tasted good too.

At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

S.FORM: apakakanenganai kodhali, mani oanai talatengae ketekete aa'o.

S.TRANSL: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

S.FORM: dhaacengalrao iyalrailidhe, amo kaava'ingalrainome cengele iyalrailidhe.

S.TRANSL: 我會怕蛇，所以我不看蛇類。
I'm afraid of snakes, so I don't look at snakes.

I saw a lot of birds flying around in the trees and saw owls sleeping in tree hollows.

S.FORM: madhao posasingaeli, amo pacengelrao linali.

I took a lot of pictures and showed them to my parents.

Next time I will come with my parents.

This holiday, my sister and I went to Taitung to visit my uncle.

S.TRANSL: 到了台東，叔叔開車來車站接我們。

The station is not far from my uncle's house, and it's just a short drive away.

S.TRANSL: 叔叔家附近有個很大的牧場，我們一起騎腳踏車到牧場。
There is a big ranch near my uncle's house, and we rode bicycles to the ranch together.

S.FORM: ikinai talapolaopoe 'olrao, amali 'adhini aviliname moa pakane kii'i la 'anolrihinai.

S.TRANSL: 在牧場，叔叔帶我們餵羊和騎馬。
In the ranch, my uncle took us to feed the sheep and ride horses.

S.FORM: maongonae, mani kahedhelenai moa sechi kone ko'ovo.

S.TRANSL: 晚上，我們一起去市區吃飯。

There are a lot of cars in the city, so we go there by motorcycle.

When we were about to leave, we found that the motorcycle had been stolen, so we had to leave by bus.

S.FORM: 'inanga 'a 'oilringanai moa taihoko, olriho'okata amali 'adhini o'otomongakadha 'otobaidha.

S.TRANSL: 現在我們回到台北了，不知道叔叔的摩托車找到了沒有。
Now that we are back in Taipei, I don't know if my uncle's motorcycle has been found.

S.FORM: akalrolaili dha'anenai omiki 'oponoho, asolasolatengali mani, 'aelanai moa hiito, mahadhaolonga miki lrevake siikace.

S.PHON: aoaŋali ðaigako, mani piɭiɭao ta kaəaəa kaoʦaə boŋka ʔi solatə, taʔasaɭao ɭioʔo taiwan kaoʦaə ta tohamoho maʔakaələla taiʔaəni boŋka.
S.FORM: aoangali dhaigako, mani pilrilrao ta kaeaea kaocae bongka 'i solate, ta'asalrao lrio'o taiwan kaocae ta tohamoho ma'akaelela tai'aeni bongka.
S.PHON: aoaŋali ðaigako, mani piɭiɭao ta kaəaəa kaoʦaə boŋka ʔi solatə, taʔasaɭao ɭioʔo taiwan kaoʦaə ta tohamoho maʔakaələla taiʔaəni boŋka.
S.TRANSL: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

S.FORM: to'atolohaeli, 'ina mene caili kalalrapa'ae taivalrivalriyae, ilraae la takali 'aolai kaava'inai movalriyo 'itovolrovolro ta kaahodhange, nai lriho'o toce'elao zokonai bongka iyanai.
S.PHON: toʔatolohaəli, ʔina mənə ʦaili kalaɭapaʔaə taivaɭivaɭijaə, iɭaaə la takali ʔaolai kaavaʔinai movaɭijo ʔitovoɭovoɭo ta kaahoðaŋə, nai ɭihoʔo toʦəʔəlao zokonai boŋka ijanai.
S.FORM: to'atolohaeli, 'ina mene caili kalalrapa'ae taivalrivalriyae, ilraae la takali 'aolai kaava'inai movalriyo 'itovolrovolro ta kaahodhange, nai lriho'o toce'elao zokonai bongka iyanai.
S.PHON: toʔatolohaəli, ʔina mənə ʦaili kalaɭapaʔaə taivaɭivaɭijaə, iɭaaə la takali ʔaolai kaavaʔinai movaɭijo ʔitovoɭovoɭo ta kaahoðaŋə, nai ɭihoʔo toʦəʔəlao zokonai boŋka ijanai.
S.TRANSL: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

S.FORM: ta kahodhange otahamavalrolonga caili, lalo cengelemaidhe i'ana ta icengecenge make'ecenge ocaodha.
S.PHON: ta kahoðaŋə otahamavaɭoloŋa ʦaili, lalo ʦəŋələmaiðə iʔana ta iʦəŋəʦəŋə makəʔəʦəŋə oʦaoða.
S.FORM: ta kahodhange otahamavalrolonga caili, lalo cengelemaidhe i'ana ta icengecenge make'ecenge ocaodha.
S.PHON: ta kahoðaŋə otahamavaɭoloŋa ʦaili, lalo ʦəŋələmaiðə iʔana ta iʦəŋəʦəŋə makəʔəʦəŋə oʦaoða.
S.TRANSL: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

S.FORM: mani 'itovolrolraidhe kavahadha alriho'ae'o, alriho'aedha macaci'o 'a 'oponoho vaha, la lriiho'o paiwan vaha, bonong vaha la nipong vaha.
S.PHON: mani ʔitovoɭoɭaiðə kavahaða aɭihoʔaəʔo, aɭihoʔaəða maʦaʦiʔo ʔa ʔoponoho vaha, la ɭiihoʔo paiwan vaha, bonoŋ vaha la nipoŋ vaha.
S.FORM: mani 'itovolrolraidhe kavahadha alriho'ae'o, alriho'aedha macaci'o 'a 'oponoho vaha, la lriiho'o paiwan vaha, bonong vaha la nipong vaha.
S.PHON: mani ʔitovoɭoɭaiðə kavahaða aɭihoʔaəʔo, aɭihoʔaəða maʦaʦiʔo ʔa ʔoponoho vaha, la ɭiihoʔo paiwan vaha, bonoŋ vaha la nipoŋ vaha.
S.TRANSL: 我問他會哪些語言，他的萬山語說得很流利，也會說排灣語、布農族話和日本話。
I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in 'oponoho, as well as Paiwan, Bunun and Japanese.

S.FORM: a'itovolrongalidhe ta'asalrao lriho'o, akalrolaidha 'a 'ipato'oto'onga nipong vaha, la lamengaedha 'a tali paiwan ocao, la 'ihakii taikiedha 'asika'olo mani palrihilriho'o tali bongo ocao, to'atolohaedha lriho'o kapa vahanga.
S.PHON: aʔitovoɭoŋaliðə taʔasaɭao ɭihoʔo, akaɭolaiða ʔa ʔipatoʔotoʔoŋa nipoŋ vaha, la laməŋaəða ʔa tali paiwan oʦao, la ʔihakii taikiəða ʔasikaʔolo mani paɭihiɭihoʔo tali boŋo oʦao, toʔatolohaəða ɭihoʔo kapa vahaŋa.
S.FORM: a'itovolrongalidhe ta'asalrao lriho'o, akalrolaidha 'a 'ipato'oto'onga nipong vaha, la lamengaedha 'a tali paiwan ocao, la 'ihakii taikiedha 'asika'olo mani palrihilriho'o tali bongo ocao, to'atolohaedha lriho'o kapa vahanga.
S.PHON: aʔitovoɭoŋaliðə taʔasaɭao ɭihoʔo, akaɭolaiða ʔa ʔipatoʔotoʔoŋa nipoŋ vaha, la laməŋaəða ʔa tali paiwan oʦao, la ʔihakii taikiəða ʔasikaʔolo mani paɭihiɭihoʔo tali boŋo oʦao, toʔatolohaəða ɭihoʔo kapa vahaŋa.
S.TRANSL: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是排灣族人，而他工作上認識許多布農族人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Paiwan, and he knows many Bunun at work, so he can speak so many languages.

S.FORM: ona a'itovolronaidhe, dhona ta kahodhange pato'iname ta kadhao 'aamadhalae tapapi'aeni ta alopo.
S.PHON: ona aʔitovoɭonaiðə, ðona ta kahoðaŋə patoʔinamə ta kaðao ʔaamaðalaə tapapiʔaəni ta alopo.
S.FORM: ona a'itovolronaidhe, dhona ta kahodhange pato'iname ta kadhao 'aamadhalae tapapi'aeni ta alopo.
S.PHON: ona aʔitovoɭonaiðə, ðona ta kahoðaŋə patoʔinamə ta kaðao ʔaamaðalaə tapapiʔaəni ta alopo.
S.TRANSL: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

S.FORM: topodhaolae ka poelrekanai lriho'o sialalra, oha'eve la to'ahakinai soci lo'ing, dholromao 'oelaanga toce'elao siyalara.
S.PHON: topoðaolaə ka poəɭəkanai ɭihoʔo sialaɭa, ohaʔəvə la toʔahakinai soʦi loʔiŋ, ðoɭomao ʔoəlaaŋa toʦəʔəlao sijalara.
S.FORM: topodhaolae ka poelrekanai lriho'o sialalra, oha'eve la to'ahakinai soci lo'ing, dholromao 'oelaanga toce'elao siyalara.
S.PHON: topoðaolaə ka poəɭəkanai ɭihoʔo sialaɭa, ohaʔəvə la toʔahakinai soʦi loʔiŋ, ðoɭomao ʔoəlaaŋa toʦəʔəlao sijalara.
S.TRANSL: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
Although we could't fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

S.FORM: akalrolaili matilriho'okali ka zokonai bongkani, ilraae kaocaelrao lo miyali 'akoka'iniyae.
S.PHON: akaɭolaili matiɭihoʔokali ka zokonai boŋkani, iɭaaə kaoʦaəɭao lo mijali ʔakokaʔinijaə.
S.FORM: akalrolaili matilriho'okali ka zokonai bongkani, ilraae kaocaelrao lo miyali 'akoka'iniyae.
S.PHON: akaɭolaili matiɭihoʔokali ka zokonai boŋkani, iɭaaə kaoʦaəɭao lo mijali ʔakokaʔinijaə.
S.TRANSL: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
When I was young, I didn't understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

S.FORM: alriho'ongali kinatodhaoenga, mani 'inyaiyangalrao 'ina 'oponoholra ocao, 'a maiconganae tai'aeni iyalrao, 'initatoelaelangalrao.
S.PHON: aɭihoʔoŋali kinatoðaoəŋa, mani ʔinjaijaŋaɭao ʔina ʔoponohoɭa oʦao, ʔa maiʦoŋanaə taiʔaəni ijaɭao, ʔinitatoəlaəlaŋaɭao.
S.FORM: alriho'ongali kinatodhaoenga, mani 'inyaiyangalrao 'ina 'oponoholra ocao, 'a maiconganae tai'aeni iyalrao, 'initatoelaelangalrao.
S.PHON: aɭihoʔoŋali kinatoðaoəŋa, mani ʔinjaijaŋaɭao ʔina ʔoponohoɭa oʦao, ʔa maiʦoŋanaə taiʔaəni ijaɭao, ʔinitatoəlaəlaŋaɭao.
S.TRANSL: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為萬山族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
The more I know, the more I feel that being a 'oponoho is unique and something to be proud of.

S.FORM: mene lo 'akokadhalremedhemengai, 'adhili 'aolai amo ovaliyonga.
S.PHON: mənə lo ʔakokaðaɭəməðəməŋai, ʔaðili ʔaolai amo ovalijoŋa.
S.FORM: mene lo 'akokadhalremedhemengai, 'adhili 'aolai amo ovaliyonga.
S.PHON: mənə lo ʔakokaðaɭəməðəməŋai, ʔaðili ʔaolai amo ovalijoŋa.
S.TRANSL: 今天傍晚，弟弟就要回家了。
My younger brother is going home this evening.

S.FORM: ilraae la omoli 'avai omoanai secange longai tahokoko, oaha'anai taadhi'inga ko'ovo dhomate ladhakane 'adhili 'aolai miya.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la omoli ʔavai omoanai səʦaŋə loŋai tahokoko, oahaʔanai taaðiʔiŋa koʔovo ðomatə laðakanə ʔaðili ʔaolai mija.
S.FORM: ilraae la omoli 'avai omoanai secange longai tahokoko, oaha'anai taadhi'inga ko'ovo dhomate ladhakane 'adhili 'aolai miya.
S.PHON: iɭaaə la omoli ʔavai omoanai səʦaŋə loŋai tahokoko, oahaʔanai taaðiʔiŋa koʔovo ðomatə laðakanə ʔaðili ʔaolai mija.
S.TRANSL: 我和奶奶去市場買雞，準備煮好吃的晚餐給弟弟。
My grandma and I went to the market to buy chickens and prepared a delicious dinner for my younger brother.

S.FORM: olrivatenai talangalangalrae aa'o, talangalangalrae aa'o madhao ta lredhelredhe 'iya'aoen a aa'o.
S.PHON: oɭivatənai talaŋalaŋaɭaə aaʔo, talaŋalaŋaɭaə aaʔo maðao ta ɭəðəɭəðə ʔijaʔaoən a aaʔo.
S.FORM: olrivatenai talangalangalrae aa'o, talangalangalrae aa'o madhao ta lredhelredhe 'iya'aoen a aa'o.
S.PHON: oɭivatənai talaŋalaŋaɭaə aaʔo, talaŋalaŋaɭaə aaʔo maðao ta ɭəðəɭəðə ʔijaʔaoən a aaʔo.
S.TRANSL: 我們經過了菜攤，菜攤有好多新鮮的青菜。
We passed the vegetable stand, which had a lot of fresh green vegetables.

S.FORM: olangainai kongsincai, talaace la dhaikongo.
S.PHON: olaŋainai koŋsinʦai, talaaʦə la ðaikoŋo.
S.FORM: olangainai kongsincai, talaace la dhaikongo.
S.PHON: olaŋainai koŋsinʦai, talaaʦə la ðaikoŋo.
S.TRANSL: 我們買了空心菜、山藥和胡蘿蔔。
We bought water spinach, yams and carrots.

S.FORM: vila'a ta langalangai madho laopange mani iya 'ina, "pangodhaleli la mimilriki malimeme. kai longalra!"
S.PHON: vilaʔa ta laŋalaŋai maðo laopaŋə mani ija ʔina, "paŋoðaləli la mimiɭiki maliməmə. kai loŋaɭa!"
S.FORM: vila'a ta langalangai madho laopange mani iya 'ina, "pangodhaleli la mimilriki malimeme. kai longalra!"
S.PHON: vilaʔa ta laŋalaŋai maðo laopaŋə mani ija ʔina, "paŋoðaləli la mimiɭiki maliməmə. kai loŋaɭa!"
S.TRANSL: 旁邊水果攤的老闆說:＂我的鳳梨和桃子很甜。買一些吧!＂
The owner of the nearby fruit stand said: "My pineapples and peaches are very sweet. Buy some!"

S.FORM: olangainganai pangodhale la mimilriki.
S.PHON: olaŋaiŋanai paŋoðalə la mimiɭiki.
S.FORM: olangainganai pangodhale la mimilriki.
S.PHON: olaŋaiŋanai paŋoðalə la mimiɭiki.
S.TRANSL: 我們買了鳳梨和桃子。
We bought pineapples and peaches.

S.FORM: olrivatenai ta langalangai tahokoko, laopange mani iya 'ina, "kanani langalra'o? mene tahokoko matoalrai la ka'ima'a, ta kaeaea matolro idhai."
S.PHON: oɭivatənai ta laŋalaŋai tahokoko, laopaŋə mani ija ʔina, "kanani laŋaɭaʔo? mənə tahokoko matoaɭai la kaʔimaʔa, ta kaəaəa matoɭo iðai."
S.FORM: olrivatenai ta langalangai tahokoko, laopange mani iya 'ina, "kanani langalra'o? mene tahokoko matoalrai la ka'ima'a, ta kaeaea matolro idhai."
S.PHON: oɭivatənai ta laŋalaŋai tahokoko, laopaŋə mani ija ʔina, "kanani laŋaɭaʔo? mənə tahokoko matoaɭai la kaʔimaʔa, ta kaəaəa matoɭo iðai."
S.TRANSL: 我們經過了肉攤，老闆說:＂你要買什麼?今天的雞又大又肥，一隻300元。＂
We passed the meat stall, and the boss said, "What do you want to buy? Today's chickens are big and fat, and one is 300 NTD."

S.FORM: omoli 'avai makocingai mani iya, 'itovolro laopange kai 'akotomelre'a iya.
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai makoʦiŋai mani ija, ʔitovoɭo laopaŋə kai ʔakotoməɭəʔa ija.
S.FORM: omoli 'avai makocingai mani iya, 'itovolro laopange kai 'akotomelre'a iya.
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai makoʦiŋai mani ija, ʔitovoɭo laopaŋə kai ʔakotoməɭəʔa ija.
S.TRANSL: 奶奶覺得太貴了，問老闆可不可以便宜一點。
Grandma thought it was too expensive and asked the boss if it could be cheaper.

S.FORM: la laopange mani iya 'ina tomelre'engalrao, dhoolrongakai ka 'oalralra.
S.PHON: la laopaŋə mani ija ʔina toməɭəʔəŋaɭao, ðooɭoŋakai ka ʔoaɭaɭa.
S.FORM: la laopange mani iya 'ina tomelre'engalrao, dhoolrongakai ka 'oalralra.
S.PHON: la laopaŋə mani ija ʔina toməɭəʔəŋaɭao, ðooɭoŋakai ka ʔoaɭaɭa.
S.TRANSL: 但老闆說已經很便宜，不能再減了。
But the boss said that it is already very cheap and cannot be reduced any further.

S.FORM: ta ivila'a laopange iya 'ina, longalra ka'angeli, ka'angeli 'a matoalrai la kamelree'e.
S.PHON: ta ivilaʔa laopaŋə ija ʔina, loŋaɭa kaʔaŋəli, kaʔaŋəli ʔa matoaɭai la kaməɭəəʔə.
S.FORM: ta ivila'a laopange iya 'ina, longalra ka'angeli, ka'angeli 'a matoalrai la kamelree'e.
S.PHON: ta ivilaʔa laopaŋə ija ʔina, loŋaɭa kaʔaŋəli, kaʔaŋəli ʔa matoaɭai la kaməɭəəʔə.
S.TRANSL: 隔壁的老闆說，來買我的魚，我的魚又大又便宜。
The boss next door said, come and buy my fish, my fish is big and cheap.

S.FORM: vilrilaenga olangainai tahokoko laangainai ka'ange, 'oilringanai oha'a paso'ete cokoi 'adhamai.
S.PHON: viɭilaəŋa olaŋainai tahokoko laaŋainai kaʔaŋə, ʔoiɭiŋanai ohaʔa pasoʔətə ʦokoi ʔaðamai.
S.FORM: vilrilaenga olangainai tahokoko laangainai ka'ange, 'oilringanai oha'a paso'ete cokoi 'adhamai.
S.PHON: viɭilaəŋa olaŋainai tahokoko laaŋainai kaʔaŋə, ʔoiɭiŋanai ohaʔa pasoʔətə ʦokoi ʔaðamai.
S.TRANSL: 最後我們買了雞也買了魚，回家煮了一桌子的菜。
In the end we bought chicken and fish, and went home to cook a table of food.

S.FORM: ohi'adha'adhaonai, icodhalra'anga madhalremedheme.
S.PHON: ohiʔaðaʔaðaonai, iʦoðaɭaʔaŋa maðaɭəməðəmə.
S.FORM: ohi'adha'adhaonai, icodhalra'anga madhalremedheme.
S.PHON: ohiʔaðaʔaðaonai, iʦoðaɭaʔaŋa maðaɭəməðəmə.
S.TRANSL: 等了好久，天已經黑了。
After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

S.FORM: 'adhili 'aolai mani lingilingi miya 'ina, hitai apakacokai kidholro ka paovalriyo, oonai lo niciyoobinga lamo dholro maava'i.
S.PHON: ʔaðili ʔaolai mani liŋiliŋi mija ʔina, hitai apakaʦokai kiðoɭo ka paovaɭijo, oonai lo niʦijoobiŋa lamo ðoɭo maavaʔi.
S.FORM: 'adhili 'aolai mani lingilingi miya 'ina, hitai apakacokai kidholro ka paovalriyo, oonai lo niciyoobinga lamo dholro maava'i.
S.PHON: ʔaðili ʔaolai mani liŋiliŋi mija ʔina, hitai apakaʦokai kiðoɭo ka paovaɭijo, oonai lo niʦijoobiŋa lamo ðoɭo maavaʔi.
S.TRANSL: 弟弟打電話回來說，軍隊突然不能放假了，要下一個禮拜日才回來。
The younger brother called back and said that the army suddenly couldn't take a vacation and would not be back until the next Sunday.

S.FORM: omoli 'avai mani iya 'ina, oaha'angamita todhao, imiya ta cekelae 'inyaelre kone!
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai mani ija ʔina, oahaʔaŋamita toðao, imija ta ʦəkəlaə ʔinjaəɭə konə!
S.FORM: omoli 'avai mani iya 'ina, oaha'angamita todhao, imiya ta cekelae 'inyaelre kone!
S.PHON: omoli ʔavai mani ija ʔina, oahaʔaŋamita toðao, imija ta ʦəkəlaə ʔinjaəɭə konə!
S.TRANSL: 奶奶說已經煮了這麼多，叫大家一起來吃吧!
Grandma said that she has cooked so much, so let's eat together!

S.FORM: mani alraalraalranai palrapalra la la'ivi'ivokonai, 'iniyaelrenai kone ta tohamoho madhao ko'ovo.
S.PHON: mani aɭaaɭaaɭanai paɭapaɭa la laʔiviʔivokonai, ʔinijaəɭənai konə ta tohamoho maðao koʔovo.
S.FORM: mani alraalraalranai palrapalra la la'ivi'ivokonai, 'iniyaelrenai kone ta tohamoho madhao ko'ovo.
S.PHON: mani aɭaaɭaaɭanai paɭapaɭa la laʔiviʔivokonai, ʔinijaəɭənai konə ta tohamoho maðao koʔovo.
S.TRANSL: 我們邀請了親朋好友，一起來享用豐盛的晚餐。
We invited friends and family to have a great dinner together.

S.FORM: omiki elreelreli vaaho omiidhe, kama ahakahimoho omece 'ahakikidha.
S.PHON: omiki əɭəəɭəli vaaho omiiðə, kama ahakahimoho oməʦə ʔahakikiða.
S.FORM: omiki elreelreli vaaho omiidhe, kama ahakahimoho omece 'ahakikidha.
S.PHON: omiki əɭəəɭəli vaaho omiiðə, kama ahakahimoho oməʦə ʔahakikiða.
S.TRANSL: 我有一個同學叫Vaaho，他常常忘記帶東西。
I have a classmate named Vaaho, he often forgets to bring things.

S.FORM: lo solatedha, mani 'ipasolriya elreelreedha inpice, 'acocaocao la tapikikiyae solate.
S.PHON: lo solatəða, mani ʔipasoɭija əɭəəɭəəða inpiʦə, ʔaʦoʦaoʦao la tapikikijaə solatə.
S.FORM: lo solatedha, mani 'ipasolriya elreelreedha inpice, 'acocaocao la tapikikiyae solate.
S.PHON: lo solatəða, mani ʔipasoɭija əɭəəɭəəða inpiʦə, ʔaʦoʦaoʦao la tapikikijaə solatə.
S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，他跟同學借鉛筆、橡皮擦或是筆記本。
During class, he borrowed pencils, erasers or notebooks from his classmates.

S.FORM: 'oale, 'opo'ale, 'otolrolo, cekelaenaidhe pasolriya 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: ʔoalə, ʔopoʔalə, ʔotoɭolo, ʦəkəlaənaiðə pasoɭija ʔahakiki.
S.FORM: 'oale, 'opo'ale, 'otolrolo, cekelaenaidhe pasolriya 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: ʔoalə, ʔopoʔalə, ʔotoɭolo, ʦəkəlaənaiðə pasoɭija ʔahakiki.
S.TRANSL: 一次、兩次、三次，大家會借東西給他。
Once, twice, three times, everyone would lend him something.

S.FORM: oha'eve la omiki takatakolraedha ta to'inilrao, lo 'ipasolriyadha ka olriho'okadha ka 'oilri.
S.PHON: ohaʔəvə la omiki takatakoɭaəða ta toʔiniɭao, lo ʔipasoɭijaða ka oɭihoʔokaða ka ʔoiɭi.

S.TRANSL: 但是他有個壞習慣，經常借了東西卻不還。
But he has a bad habit of often borrowing things but not returning them.

S.FORM: vilrilaenga cekelaenai kipasoolriyangidhe 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: viɭilaəŋa ʦəkəlaənai kipasooɭijaŋiðə ʔahakiki.
S.FORM: vilrilaenga cekelaenai kipasoolriyangidhe 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: viɭilaəŋa ʦəkəlaənai kipasooɭijaŋiðə ʔahakiki.
S.TRANSL: 最後大家都不想借他東西了。
In the end, no one wanted to borrow anything from him.

S.FORM: iiki ta kaeaea menane, cekelaenai molatadhe 'oliliyo.
S.PHON: iiki ta kaəaəa mənanə, ʦəkəlaənai molataðə ʔolilijo.
S.FORM: iiki ta kaeaea menane, cekelaenai molatadhe 'oliliyo.
S.PHON: iiki ta kaəaəa mənanə, ʦəkəlaənai molataðə ʔolilijo.
S.TRANSL: 有一天，我們大家一起去郊遊。
One day, we all went on an outing together.

S.FORM: tavalrevalre'anae, vaaho 'api otavilavila'ae, la kahimoho omece oisengce.
S.PHON: tavaɭəvaɭəʔanaə, vaaho ʔapi otavilavilaʔaə, la kahimoho oməʦə oisəŋʦə.
S.FORM: tavalrevalre'anae, vaaho 'api otavilavila'ae, la kahimoho omece oisengce.
S.PHON: tavaɭəvaɭəʔanaə, vaaho ʔapi otavilavilaʔaə, la kahimoho oməʦə oisəŋʦə.
S.TRANSL: 路途中，Vaaho想上廁所，但他忘記帶衛生紙。
On the way, Kacaw wanted to go to the bathroom, but he forgot to bring toilet paper.

S.FORM: mani 'ipasolriya elreelreedha oisengce, la cekelaenai miidhe 'ina, oameecekali.
S.PHON: mani ʔipasoɭija əɭəəɭəəða oisəŋʦə, la ʦəkəlaənai miiðə ʔina, oaməəʦəkali.
S.FORM: mani 'ipasolriya elreelreedha oisengce, la cekelaenai miidhe 'ina, oameecekali.

S.TRANSL: 他跟同學借衛生紙，可是大家都回他說，我也沒有帶耶。
He borrowed toilet paper from his classmates, but everyone told him that I didn't bring them either.

S.FORM: vaaho pokoali iya tovi. mani kasekelre miya 'ina, kani cekelaaenomi, kipasolriiyae oisengce!
S.PHON: vaaho pokoali ija tovi. mani kasəkəɭə mija ʔina, kani ʦəkəlaaənomi, kipasoɭiijaə oisəŋʦə!
S.FORM: vaaho pokoali iya tovi. mani kasekelre miya 'ina, kani cekelaaenomi, kipasolriiyae oisengce!
S.PHON: vaaho pokoali ija tovi. mani kasəkəɭə mija ʔina, kani ʦəkəlaaənomi, kipasoɭiijaə oisəŋʦə!
S.TRANSL: Vaaho急的哭了出來。他生氣的說，為什麼大家都不借我衛生紙!
Vaaho burst into tears. He said angrily, why don't everyone lend me toilet paper!

S.FORM: elreelreedha mani pato'idhe, olriho'oka'o ka 'iniyamece, lo pasolriyanaimiya'e kilriho'ongamo'o ka 'oilri!
S.PHON: əɭəəɭəəða mani patoʔiðə, oɭihoʔokaʔo ka ʔinijaməʦə, lo pasoɭijanaimijaʔə kiɭihoʔoŋamoʔo ka ʔoiɭi!
S.FORM: elreelreedha mani pato'idhe, olriho'oka'o ka 'iniyamece, lo pasolriyanaimiya'e kilriho'ongamo'o ka 'oilri!
S.PHON: əɭəəɭəəða mani patoʔiðə, oɭihoʔokaʔo ka ʔinijaməʦə, lo pasoɭijanaimijaʔə kiɭihoʔoŋamoʔo ka ʔoiɭi!
S.TRANSL: 同學告訴他，因為你都不自己帶，而且借給你的東西，你都不會還!
The classmate told him, because you didn't bring it yourself, and you wouldn't return the things lent to you!

S.FORM: 'idhanaenga aoadha gako, vaaho 'anolake'enga moa gako, vaaho 'ipasolriyaedha elreelredha mani poelrenga 'oilri voa'i elreelreedha.
S.PHON: ʔiðanaəŋa aoaða gako, vaaho ʔanolakəʔəŋa moa gako, vaaho ʔipasoɭijaəða əɭəəɭəða mani poəɭəŋa ʔoiɭi voaʔi əɭəəɭəəða.
S.FORM: 'idhanaenga aoadha gako, vaaho 'anolake'enga moa gako, vaaho 'ipasolriyaedha elreelredha mani poelrenga 'oilri voa'i elreelreedha.
S.PHON: ʔiðanaəŋa aoaða gako, vaaho ʔanolakəʔəŋa moa gako, vaaho ʔipasoɭijaəða əɭəəɭəða mani poəɭəŋa ʔoiɭi voaʔi əɭəəɭəəða.
S.TRANSL: 隔天上學，Vaaho很早就到學校了，Vaaho把跟同學借的東西都還給同學們。
The next day at school, Vaaho arrived at school early and returned everything he had borrowed from his classmates.

S.FORM: vaaho mani liicinganomi iya elreelreedha, matalaki mani iya 'ina lo vilrilaenga amo 'iniyamecengalrao 'ahakikili, icongonganomi elreelreeli 'amadhalae dholronomi pasolriyae 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: vaaho mani liiʦiŋanomi ija əɭəəɭəəða, matalaki mani ija ʔina lo viɭilaəŋa amo ʔinijaməʦəŋaɭao ʔahakikili, iʦoŋoŋanomi əɭəəɭəəli ʔamaðalaə ðoɭonomi pasoɭijaə ʔahakiki.
S.FORM: vaaho mani liicinganomi iya elreelreedha, matalaki mani iya 'ina lo vilrilaenga amo 'iniyamecengalrao 'ahakikili, icongonganomi elreelreeli 'amadhalae dholronomi pasolriyae 'ahakiki.
S.PHON: vaaho mani liiʦiŋanomi ija əɭəəɭəəða, matalaki mani ija ʔina lo viɭilaəŋa amo ʔinijaməʦəŋaɭao ʔahakikili, iʦoŋoŋanomi əɭəəɭəəli ʔamaðalaə ðoɭonomi pasoɭijaə ʔahakiki.
S.TRANSL: Vaaho向同學們道歉，並說他以後一定會自己帶東西，也謝謝同學們之前願意借他東西。
Vaaho apologized to the students and said that he would definitely bring his own things in the future, and thanked the classmates for their willingness to lend him things before.

S.FORM: amali 'a valriyonai ta aloalopo.
S.PHON: amali ʔa vaɭijonai ta aloalopo.
S.FORM: amali 'a valriyonai ta aloalopo.
S.PHON: amali ʔa vaɭijonai ta aloalopo.
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

S.FORM: ona akalrolainli, amali la 'adhini 'aolai lo maongo molatadhe, 'ikoange la 'iya'alodho.
S.PHON: ona akaɭolainli, amali la ʔaðini ʔaolai lo maoŋo molataðə, ʔikoaŋə la ʔijaʔaloðo.
S.FORM: ona akalrolainli, amali la 'adhini 'aolai lo maongo molatadhe, 'ikoange la 'iya'alodho.
S.PHON: ona akaɭolainli, amali la ʔaðini ʔaolai lo maoŋo molataðə, ʔikoaŋə la ʔijaʔaloðo.
S.TRANSL: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上出門，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

S.FORM: malehengai, mani ovalriyo omece alralini alopae.
S.PHON: maləhəŋai, mani ovaɭijo oməʦə aɭalini alopaə.
S.FORM: malehengai, mani ovalriyo omece alralini alopae.
S.PHON: maləhəŋai, mani ovaɭijo oməʦə aɭalini alopaə.
S.TRANSL: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

S.FORM: dhomanae omiki vavoi, iiki lraa, omikii lo mikidha. tikohai.
S.PHON: ðomanaə omiki vavoi, iiki ɭaa, omikii lo mikiða. tikohai.
S.FORM: dhomanae omiki vavoi, iiki lraa, omikii lo mikidha. tikohai.

S.TRANSL: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會有雉雞。
Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

S.FORM: taesa miki valriyo tai'adha'adhae apa'aavinga alopae, mani apa'avi voa'i cinekelae.
S.PHON: taəsa miki vaɭijo taiʔaðaʔaðaə apaʔaaviŋa alopaə, mani apaʔavi voaʔi ʦinəkəlaə.
S.FORM: taesa miki valriyo tai'adha'adhae apa'aavinga alopae, mani apa'avi voa'i cinekelae.
S.PHON: taəsa miki vaɭijo taiʔaðaʔaðaə apaʔaaviŋa alopaə, mani apaʔavi voaʔi ʦinəkəlaə.
S.TRANSL: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

S.FORM: taesalrao makaalai amali, nao 'anopapalra olopo miya.
S.PHON: taəsaɭao makaalai amali, nao ʔanopapaɭa olopo mija.
S.FORM: taesalrao makaalai amali, nao 'anopapalra olopo miya.
S.PHON: taəsaɭao makaalai amali, nao ʔanopapaɭa olopo mija.
S.TRANSL: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

S.FORM: mani iyae amali 'ina onailo maolohonga'o amo avililraimiya'e.
S.PHON: mani ijaə amali ʔina onailo maolohoŋaʔo amo aviliɭaimijaʔə.
S.FORM: mani iyae amali 'ina onailo maolohonga'o amo avililraimiya'e.
S.PHON: mani ijaə amali ʔina onailo maolohoŋaʔo amo aviliɭaimijaʔə.
S.TRANSL: 爸爸說等我長大了他再帶我一起去。
Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

S.FORM: valriyonai a'olalai olrihoo'o tokoho alopae, olriho'o to'akelrecae la totangetange tokoho.
S.PHON: vaɭijonai aʔolalai oɭihooʔo tokoho alopaə, oɭihoʔo toʔakəɭəʦaə la totaŋətaŋə tokoho.
S.FORM: valriyonai a'olalai olrihoo'o tokoho alopae, olriho'o to'akelrecae la totangetange tokoho.
S.PHON: vaɭijonai aʔolalai oɭihooʔo tokoho alopaə, oɭihoʔo toʔakəɭəʦaə la totaŋətaŋə tokoho.
S.TRANSL: 部落的男人也會用陷阱抓獵物，他們會製作套索陷阱和石壓式陷阱。
The men of the tribe also trap their prey, making lasso traps and rock traps.

S.FORM: amali malrihili to'akelrecae tokoho, amali omiya 'ina, 'ina 'a 'atokoho tikohai, amo pa'iniyacehaemita cengele alopae dhakaseni, lo tokohomao opikimao 'oloni alopae.
S.PHON: amali maɭihili toʔakəɭəʦaə tokoho, amali omija ʔina, ʔina ʔa ʔatokoho tikohai, amo paʔinijaʦəhaəmita ʦəŋələ alopaə ðakasəni, lo tokohomao opikimao ʔoloni alopaə.
S.FORM: amali malrihili to'akelrecae tokoho, amali omiya 'ina, 'ina 'a 'atokoho tikohai, amo pa'iniyacehaemita cengele alopae dhakaseni, lo tokohomao opikimao 'oloni alopae.
S.PHON: amali maɭihili toʔakəɭəʦaə tokoho, amali omija ʔina, ʔina ʔa ʔatokoho tikohai, amo paʔinijaʦəhaəmita ʦəŋələ alopaə ðakasəni, lo tokohomao opikimao ʔoloni alopaə.
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸很會製作套索陷阱，爸爸說，這是用來捕抓雉雞的，我們要仔細觀察獵物的腳印，陷阱要放在獵物走的路上。

My dad is very good at making lasso traps. Dad said that this is used to catch pheasants. We have to carefully observe the footprints of the prey, and the traps should be placed on the path of the prey.

S.FORM: 'ihakilrao nao padhalro'o dholro 'anoaelriline atamatama olopo miya, pamenanaelrao loalala to'atokohae, ki'asaino tokoholi mani dholronga paotokoho alopae.
S.PHON: ʔihakiɭao nao paðaɭoʔo ðoɭo ʔanoaəɭilinə atamatama olopo mija, pamənanaəɭao loalala toʔatokohaə, kiʔasaino tokoholi mani ðoɭoŋa paotokoho alopaə.
S.FORM: 'ihakilrao nao padhalro'o dholro 'anoaelriline atamatama olopo miya, pamenanaelrao loalala to'atokohae, ki'asaino tokoholi mani dholronga paotokoho alopae.
S.PHON: ʔihakiɭao nao paðaɭoʔo ðoɭo ʔanoaəɭilinə atamatama olopo mija, pamənanaəɭao loalala toʔatokohaə, kiʔasaino tokoholi mani ðoɭoŋa paotokoho alopaə.
S.TRANSL: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的可以抓到獵物了。
In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

S.FORM: amali acengelengai mani iyae 'ina lo vilrilaenga amo 'anomakaelaemo'o malrihili oloalopo.
S.PHON: amali aʦəŋələŋai mani ijaə ʔina lo viɭilaəŋa amo ʔanomakaəlaəmoʔo maɭihili oloalopo.

S.TRANSL: 爸爸看到後對我說我以後一定會是很厲害的獵人。
Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

S.FORM: 'inanga maolohongalrao, mani angelengalrao vo'oli la lrailili, 'ano papalrangalro amali olopo.
S.PHON: ʔinaŋa maolohoŋaɭao, mani aŋələŋaɭao voʔoli la ɭailili, ʔano papaɭaŋaɭo amali olopo.

S.TRANSL: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
Now that I have grown up, I carry my bow and arrows and go hunting with my father.

S.FORM: aovalriyonganai mani apa'avinai alranai alopae voa'i ocao povocongo.
S.PHON: aovaɭijoŋanai mani apaʔavinai aɭanai alopaə voaʔi oʦao povoʦoŋo.

S.TRANSL: 回村時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

S.FORM: amali mani iya 'ina, amo icongongai miyamita loomo pa'apelengaimite, mani alramita todhao alopae.
S.PHON: amali mani ija ʔina, amo iʦoŋoŋai mijamita loomo paʔapələŋaimitə, mani aɭamita toðao alopaə.

S.TRANSL: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，讓我們有很多獵物收穫。
Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.

S.FORM: 'olriangalrao maelre moa gako.
S.PHON: ʔoɭiaŋaɭao maəɭə moa gako.

I was late for school this morning.

S.FORM: aoali gako 'a, marimorolrao omece kaabang.

S.FORM: aoali gako 'a, marimorolrao omece kaabang.

S.TRANSL: 去學校的時候，我忘了帶書包。
When I was going to school, I forgot to bring my backpack.

S.FORM: ikaoodho ka ameceli.

S.FORM: ikaoodho ka ameceli.
S.PHON: ikaooðo ka aməʦəli.

I didn't bring anything with me.

S.FORM: lo solatenai 'a, ta'aelrelrao elreelreli cengele solate.

S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，我和同學一起看課本。
During class, I and my classmates read the textbook together.

During the exam, a classmate lent me a pen and an eraser.

S.FORM: "'ara'apecea, lako ki'olrianga!" omiiae singsi.
S.PHON: "ʔaraʔapəʦəa, lako kiʔoɭiaŋa!" omiiaə siŋsi.

S.TRANSL: 老師跟我說：＂下次早點起床，不要再遲到了!＂
The teacher told me, "Wake up earlier next time and don't be late again!"

S.FORM: ivokoli 'a 'Esai, taliDhekai lalake.
S.PHON: ivokoli ʔa ʔəsai, taliðəkai lalakə.
S.FORM: ivokoli 'a 'Esai, taliDhekai lalake.
S.PHON: ivokoli ʔa ʔəsai, taliðəkai lalakə.
S.TRANSL: 我的好朋友叫’Esai，他也是魯凱族的小孩。
My good friend's name is 'Esai, and he is also an Rukai child.

S.FORM: ta'aelranai iki 'Oponoho solasolate.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭanai iki ʔoponoho solasolatə.
S.FORM: ta'aelranai iki 'Oponoho solasolate.
S.PHON: taʔaəɭanai iki ʔoponoho solasolatə.
S.TRANSL: 我們一起在萬山讀書。
We study together in the 'Oponoho tribe.

S.FORM: osilalalranai takaarodhange lo lomoamolailidha, la kalrihilinai 'ako'Oponohae.
S.PHON: osilalaɭanai takaaroðaŋə lo lomoamolailiða, la kaɭihilinai ʔakoʔoponohaə.
S.FORM: osilalalranai takaarodhange lo lomoamolailidha, la kalrihilinai 'ako'Oponohae.
S.PHON: osilalaɭanai takaaroðaŋə lo lomoamolailiða, la kaɭihilinai ʔakoʔoponohaə.
S.TRANSL: 我們常常一起聽耆老說故事，我們都很會說萬山話。
We often listen to the elders telling stories together, and we both can speak the 'Oponohae proficiently.

S.FORM: maka'anahalrelrao la kavotolro'o, 'Esai 'a maka'analeve la karilai.
S.PHON: makaʔanahaɭəɭao la kavotoɭoʔo, ʔəsai ʔa makaʔanaləvə la karilai.
S.FORM: maka'anahalrelrao la kavotolro'o, 'Esai 'a maka'analeve la karilai.
S.PHON: makaʔanahaɭəɭao la kavotoɭoʔo, ʔəsai ʔa makaʔanaləvə la karilai.
S.TRANSL: 我很高也很胖，’Esai很矮也很瘦。
I am tall and overweight, while 'Esai is short and thin.

S.FORM: malromihalre 'Esai la cekelaidhe ocao madhalame.
S.PHON: maɭomihaɭə ʔəsai la ʦəkəlaiðə oʦao maðalamə.
S.FORM: malromihalre 'Esai la cekelaidhe ocao madhalame.
S.PHON: maɭomihaɭə ʔəsai la ʦəkəlaiðə oʦao maðalamə.
S.TRANSL: ’Esai很有活力，大家都很喜歡他。
'Esai is very energetic, and everyone likes him a lot.

S.FORM: makahakahai mene 'i gako, madhao 'apa'ikalromalromale.
S.PHON: makahakahai mənə ʔi gako, maðao ʔapaʔikaɭomaɭomalə.
S.FORM: makahakahai mene 'i gako, madhao 'apa'ikalromalromale.
S.PHON: makahakahai mənə ʔi gako, maðao ʔapaʔikaɭomaɭomalə.
S.TRANSL: 今天是學校的運動會，學校舉辦了許多競賽。
Today was the school's sports day, and the school organized many competitions.

S.FORM: omiki mavilivilivili, zucio, lrolrame, 'odholri la pana.
S.PHON: omiki mavilivilivili, zuʦio, ɭoɭamə, ʔoðoɭi la pana.
S.FORM: omiki mavilivilivili, zucio, lrolrame, 'odholri la pana.
S.PHON: omiki mavilivilivili, zuʦio, ɭoɭamə, ʔoðoɭi la pana.
S.TRANSL: 有拔河、足球、跑步、跳舞和射箭。
There were tug of war, soccer, running, dancing, and archery.

S.FORM: ma'ikalromalromalelrao lrolrame.
S.PHON: maʔikaɭomaɭomaləɭao ɭoɭamə.
S.FORM: ma'ikalromalromalelrao lrolrame.
S.PHON: maʔikaɭomaɭomaləɭao ɭoɭamə.
S.TRANSL: 我比賽跑步。
I participated in the running competition.

S.FORM: alralrameli 'a olravelelrao mani 'inidhadhaiae 'aporehai sahaoli.
S.PHON: aɭaɭaməli ʔa oɭavələɭao mani ʔiniðaðaiaə ʔaporəhai sahaoli.
S.FORM: alralrameli 'a olravelelrao mani 'inidhadhaiae 'aporehai sahaoli.
S.PHON: aɭaɭaməli ʔa oɭavələɭao mani ʔiniðaðaiaə ʔaporəhai sahaoli.
S.TRANSL: 當我跑步的時候，我跌倒了，膝蓋流了很多血。
When I was running, I fell down, and my knees were bleeding.

S.FORM: mani 'inicekalelrao pacongolo lrolrame.
S.PHON: mani ʔiniʦəkaləɭao paʦoŋolo ɭoɭamə.
S.FORM: mani 'inicekalelrao pacongolo lrolrame.
S.PHON: mani ʔiniʦəkaləɭao paʦoŋolo ɭoɭamə.
S.TRANSL: 但我站起來，繼續向前跑。
But I stood up and continued running forward.

S.FORM: aoangali taolropengae 'a cekelaiae maraveravere ana ocao.
S.PHON: aoaŋali taoɭopəŋaə ʔa ʦəkəlaiaə maravəravərə ana oʦao.
S.FORM: aoangali taolropengae 'a cekelaiae maraveravere ana ocao.
S.PHON: aoaŋali taoɭopəŋaə ʔa ʦəkəlaiaə maravəravərə ana oʦao.
S.TRANSL: 我到終點時所有人都很開心。
Everyone was happy when I reached the finish line.

S.FORM: topodhaolae ka omalrakali ka 'akaea la maraveraverelrao, alakai pa'alropengelrao ma'ikalromale.
S.PHON: topoðaolaə ka omaɭakali ka ʔakaəa la maravəravərəɭao, alakai paʔaɭopəŋəɭao maʔikaɭomalə.
S.FORM: topodhaolae ka omalrakali ka 'akaea la maraveraverelrao, alakai pa'alropengelrao ma'ikalromale.
S.PHON: topoðaolaə ka omaɭakali ka ʔakaəa la maravəravərəɭao, alakai paʔaɭopəŋəɭao maʔikaɭomalə.
S.TRANSL: 雖然沒有獲得第一名，但我很高興，因為我完成了比賽。
Although I didn't get first place, I'm glad because I completed the race.

S.FORM: o'idha 'a 'anodhadho'alrao amali moa cengele pa'atakani amali.
S.PHON: oʔiða ʔa ʔanoðaðoʔaɭao amali moa ʦəŋələ paʔatakani amali.

S.PHON: oʔiða ʔa ʔanoðaðoʔaɭao amali moa ʦəŋələ paʔatakani amali.
S.TRANSL: 昨天我和爸爸去拜訪我的叔叔。
Yesterday, my father and I visited my uncle.

S.FORM: 'odhalri'i okokodha pa'atakani amali.
S.PHON: ʔoðaɭiʔi okokoða paʔatakani amali.
S.FORM: 'odhalri'i okokodha pa'atakani amali.
S.PHON: ʔoðaɭiʔi okokoða paʔatakani amali.
S.TRANSL: 叔叔的腳受傷了。
My uncle injured his foot.

S.FORM: mani pato'iname, "mavolroto calrai." mia.
S.PHON: mani patoʔinamə, "mavoɭoto ʦaɭai." mia.
S.FORM: mani pato'iname, "mavolroto calrai." mia.
S.PHON: mani patoʔinamə, "mavoɭoto ʦaɭai." mia.
S.TRANSL: 他告訴我們，房子的屋頂破了一個洞。
He told us that there was a hole in the roof of his house.

S.FORM: a'aelaedha calrai 'a mani 'aliki calrai 'inipalrio.
S.PHON: aʔaəlaəða ʦaɭai ʔa mani ʔaliki ʦaɭai ʔinipaɭio.
S.FORM: a'aelaedha calrai 'a mani 'aliki calrai 'inipalrio.
S.PHON: aʔaəlaəða ʦaɭai ʔa mani ʔaliki ʦaɭai ʔinipaɭio.
S.TRANSL: 他在修理屋頂時，從屋頂摔下來了。
He fell off the roof while repairing it.

S.FORM: oamece aamae votolo la saviki voa'idhe pa'atakani amali.
S.PHON: oaməʦə aamaə votolo la saviki voaʔiðə paʔatakani amali.
S.FORM: oamece aamae votolo la saviki voa'idhe pa'atakani amali.
S.PHON: oaməʦə aamaə votolo la saviki voaʔiðə paʔatakani amali.
S.TRANSL: 爸爸帶了豬肉和檳榔給叔叔。
My father brought beef and betel nuts for my uncle.

S.FORM: ma'olionai pa'atakani amali iki taikikiaedha, madhao pato'oeniae lamolai.
S.PHON: maʔolionai paʔatakani amali iki taikikiaəða, maðao patoʔoəniaə lamolai.
S.FORM: ma'olionai pa'atakani amali iki taikikiaedha, madhao pato'oeniae lamolai.
S.PHON: maʔolionai paʔatakani amali iki taikikiaəða, maðao patoʔoəniaə lamolai.
S.TRANSL: 我和叔叔在房間裡聊天，他分享了很多故事。
My uncle and I chatted in the room, and he shared many stories.

S.FORM: naodhaace aiali 'a toelaeliae pa'atakani amali, "maolohongamo'o, kinakasavaraenga." omi.
S.PHON: naoðaaʦə aiali ʔa toəlaəliaə paʔatakani amali, "maolohoŋamoʔo, kinakasavaraəŋa." omi.
S.FORM: naodhaace aiali 'a toelaeliae pa'atakani amali, "maolohongamo'o, kinakasavaraenga." omi.
S.PHON: naoðaaʦə aiali ʔa toəlaəliaə paʔatakani amali, "maolohoŋamoʔo, kinakasavaraəŋa." omi.
S.TRANSL: 我要離開前，叔叔誇我長高了，也變得更帥氣了。
Before I left, my uncle praised me for growing taller and becoming more handsome.

S.FORM: mani ialrao 'ina, "amake, lo mataadhi'ingai okoko'o 'a maava'imainame dha'anenai 'olilio."
S.PHON: mani iaɭao ʔina, "amakə, lo mataaðiʔiŋai okokoʔo ʔa maavaʔimainamə ðaʔanənai ʔolilio."
S.FORM: mani ialrao 'ina, "amake, lo mataadhi'ingai okoko'o 'a maava'imainame dha'anenai 'olilio."
S.PHON: mani iaɭao ʔina, "amakə, lo mataaðiʔiŋai okokoʔo ʔa maavaʔimainamə ðaʔanənai ʔolilio."
S.TRANSL: 我說：＂等叔叔腳好了，下次也來我們家玩。＂
I said, "When your foot gets better, come to our house next time and visit us."

S.FORM: 2022 cail, ma'olilionga siihe pataliki nibungsio oara'isolate kaocae alrace mia.
S.PHON: **** ʦail, maʔolilioŋa siihə pataliki nibuŋsio oaraʔisolatə kaoʦaə aɭaʦə mia.
S.FORM: 2022 cail, ma'olilionga siihe pataliki nibungsio oara'isolate kaocae alrace mia.
S.PHON: **** ʦail, maʔolilioŋa siihə pataliki nibuŋsio oaraʔisolatə kaoʦaə aɭaʦə mia.
S.TRANSL: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

S.FORM: 'itovolrolraine Lakadhaalae Taotao dhona tao'adhailinga takaamai 'iva'ai siihe oara'isolate kaocae alrace.
S.PHON: ʔitovoɭoɭainə lakaðaalaə taotao ðona taoʔaðailiŋa takaamai ʔivaʔai siihə oaraʔisolatə kaoʦaə aɭaʦə.
S.FORM: 'itovolrolraine Lakadhaalae Taotao dhona tao'adhailinga takaamai 'iva'ai siihe oara'isolate kaocae alrace.
S.PHON: ʔitovoɭoɭainə lakaðaalaə taotao ðona taoʔaðailiŋa takaamai ʔivaʔai siihə oaraʔisolatə kaoʦaə aɭaʦə.
S.TRANSL: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證＂單列族名＂的部落青年Lakadhaalae Taotao。
I interviewed Lakadhaalae Taotao, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

S.FORM: ma'oliongalraine 'ina niciobi amooalrao dha'anedha 'itovolro.
S.PHON: maʔolioŋaɭainə ʔina niʦiobi amooaɭao ðaʔanəða ʔitovoɭo.
S.FORM: ma'oliongalraine 'ina niciobi amooalrao dha'anedha 'itovolro.
S.PHON: maʔolioŋaɭainə ʔina niʦiobi amooaɭao ðaʔanəða ʔitovoɭo.
S.TRANSL: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

S.FORM: tapa'oliaenai 'a mani pato'iae toloraeni alracedha, mani aviliae palriho'o dha'ananaedha la valriodha.
S.PHON: tapaʔoliaənai ʔa mani patoʔiaə toloraəni aɭaʦəða, mani aviliaə paɭihoʔo ðaʔananaəða la vaɭioða.
S.FORM: tapa'oliaenai 'a mani pato'iae toloraeni alracedha, mani aviliae palriho'o dha'ananaedha la valriodha.
S.PHON: tapaʔoliaənai ʔa mani patoʔiaə toloraəni aɭaʦəða, mani aviliaə paɭihoʔo ðaʔananaəða la vaɭioða.
S.TRANSL: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

S.FORM: mani 'itovolrolraine, "kani kake'eecenge'o naopatapavalrisae 'ina alrace, matakolrakai tapapia'eni 'amadhalae?" mia.
S.PHON: mani ʔitovoɭoɭainə, "kani kakəʔəəʦəŋəʔo naopatapavaɭisaə ʔina aɭaʦə, matakoɭakai tapapiaʔəni ʔamaðalaə?" mia.
S.FORM: mani 'itovolrolraine, "kani kake'eecenge'o naopatapavalrisae 'ina alrace, matakolrakai tapapia'eni 'amadhalae?" mia.
S.PHON: mani ʔitovoɭoɭainə, "kani kakəʔəəʦəŋəʔo naopatapavaɭisaə ʔina aɭaʦə, matakoɭakai tapapiaʔəni ʔamaðalaə?" mia.
S.TRANSL: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn't the old registration method sufficient?

S.FORM: mani iiae 'ina "to'arakita Ciokoko asolataeni 'a odholrokai ka matopodhaolae palriho'o ocao, amoiiki tamopoalracidhe "la savare" mia. amookavole takataka'oma'omalrae."

S.FORM: mani iiae 'ina "to'arakita Ciokoko asolataeni 'a odholrokai ka matopodhaolae palriho'o ocao, amoiiki tamopoalracidhe "la savare" mia. amookavole takataka'oma'omalrae."
S.PHON: mani iiaə ʔina "toʔarakita ʦiokoko asolataəni ʔa oðoɭokai ka matopoðaolaə paɭihoʔo oʦao, amoiiki tamopoaɭaʦiðə "la savarə" mia. amookavolə takatakaʔomaʔomaɭaə."
S.TRANSL: \" which resulted in many jokes being made."
He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \La Xian Sheng

S.FORM: mani ia omidhe ana Lakadhaale Taotao, "'ina kaocae asolataeni 'a aanai takatopodhaolae alrace. 'ina alraceli 'a movavalre 'ina'i zokonai, ta'itoloraenai dha'ananae la takaocaeta. 'amalraine lani cekelae 'i ocao 'otomo ta'itoloraeta alrace."
S.PHON: mani ia omiðə ana lakaðaalə taotao, "ʔina kaoʦaə asolataəni ʔa aanai takatopoðaolaə aɭaʦə. ʔina aɭaʦəli ʔa movavaɭə ʔinaʔi zokonai, taʔitoloraənai ðaʔananaə la takaoʦaəta. ʔamaɭainə lani ʦəkəlaə ʔi oʦao ʔotomo taʔitoloraəta aɭaʦə."
S.FORM: mani ia omidhe ana Lakadhaale Taotao, "'ina kaocae asolataeni 'a aanai takatopodhaolae alrace. 'ina alraceli 'a movavalre 'ina'i zokonai, ta'itoloraenai dha'ananae la takaocaeta. 'amalraine lani cekelae 'i ocao 'otomo ta'itoloraeta alrace."
S.PHON: mani ia omiðə ana lakaðaalə taotao, "ʔina kaoʦaə asolataəni ʔa aanai takatopoðaolaə aɭaʦə. ʔina aɭaʦəli ʔa movavaɭə ʔinaʔi zokonai, taʔitoloraənai ðaʔananaə la takaoʦaəta. ʔamaɭainə lani ʦəkəlaə ʔi oʦao ʔotomo taʔitoloraəta aɭaʦə."
S.TRANSL: our family
Lakadhaale Taotao said, \The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group

S.FORM: 'idha 'a 'anopapalralrao oomoe moa talangalangalrae longalangai. oamece oomoe todhao alredheedhekaeni aa'o moa longalangai. mani tokane 'ina akanekanaeta kaocae voa'i ocao.
S.PHON: ʔiða ʔa ʔanopapaɭaɭao oomoə moa talaŋalaŋaɭaə loŋalaŋai. oaməʦə oomoə toðao aɭəðəəðəkaəni aaʔo moa loŋalaŋai. mani tokanə ʔina akanəkanaəta kaoʦaə voaʔi oʦao.
S.FORM: 'idha 'a 'anopapalralrao oomoe moa talangalangalrae longalangai. oamece oomoe todhao alredheedhekaeni aa'o moa longalangai. mani tokane 'ina akanekanaeta kaocae voa'i ocao.
S.PHON: ʔiða ʔa ʔanopapaɭaɭao oomoə moa talaŋalaŋaɭaə loŋalaŋai. oaməʦə oomoə toðao aɭəðəəðəkaəni aaʔo moa loŋalaŋai. mani tokanə ʔina akanəkanaəta kaoʦaə voaʔi oʦao.
S.TRANSL: 昨天我跟奶奶去市集擺攤，奶奶帶了許多自己種的蔬菜去，也製作了原住民美食給大家。
Yesterday, I went to the market with my grandmother to set up a stall. My grandmother brought a lot of vegetables that she grew herself and also prepared indigenous cuisine for everyone.

S.FORM: cekelae madhalame talangalangalraeni oomoe. madhao ocao talangai 'ama la valangato, la kadhao taoa loalaala kone aha'ani 'adhamai.
S.PHON: ʦəkəlaə maðalamə talaŋalaŋaɭaəni oomoə. maðao oʦao talaŋai ʔama la valaŋato, la kaðao taoa loalaala konə ahaʔani ʔaðamai.
S.FORM: cekelae madhalame talangalangalraeni oomoe. madhao ocao talangai 'ama la valangato, la kadhao taoa loalaala kone aha'ani 'adhamai.
S.PHON: ʦəkəlaə maðalamə talaŋalaŋaɭaəni oomoə. maðao oʦao talaŋai ʔama la valaŋato, la kaðao taoa loalaala konə ahaʔani ʔaðamai.
S.TRANSL: 大家很喜歡奶奶的攤位，龍葵和山柚很多人買，許多人來品嘗她煮的菜餚。
Everyone loved my grandmother's stall. Many people bought the \nBlack Nightshade and Manila Champereia, and many people came to taste the dishes she cooked.

S.FORM: mataadhi'i kone aha'ani lrokolrokoco la talivee'ae, la dhona alopae poa 'i karidhange to'asolro'ae. 'aanoami'ani 'a madhao valravalrae takaava'i, cekelae maava'i talangalangalraenai mahedhele.
S.PHON: mataaðiʔi konə ahaʔani ɭokoɭokoʦo la talivəəʔaə, la ðona alopaə poa ʔi kariðaŋə toʔasoɭoʔaə. ʔaanoamiʔani ʔa maðao vaɭavaɭaə takaavaʔi, ʦəkəlaə maavaʔi talaŋalaŋaɭaənai mahəðələ.
S.FORM: mataadhi'i kone aha'ani lrokolrokoco la talivee'ae, la dhona alopae poa 'i karidhange to'asolro'ae. 'aanoami'ani 'a madhao valravalrae takaava'i, cekelae maava'i talangalangalraenai mahedhele.
S.PHON: mataaðiʔi konə ahaʔani ɭokoɭokoʦo la talivəəʔaə, la ðona alopaə poa ʔi kariðaŋə toʔasoɭoʔaə. ʔaanoamiʔani ʔa maðao vaɭavaɭaə takaavaʔi, ʦəkəlaə maavaʔi talaŋalaŋaɭaənai mahəðələ.
S.TRANSL: 她煮的山蘇和米粽非常好吃，還有用山肉煮加上樹豆煮成湯，味道吸引了很多客人，大家都來我們攤位排隊。
The ferns and sticky rice she cooked were delicious, as well as the braised pork with maqaw pepper and pigeon pea soup. The flavors attracted many customers, and everyone lined up at our stall.

S.FORM: lo ohaaha'ani oomoe 'a mani ikilrao vila'a malramalramalra valravalrae, tako'iase, "'inilralredhekenai, ka posavo'okai. maava'ia pakono moa longai."
S.PHON: lo ohaahaʔani oomoə ʔa mani ikiɭao vilaʔa maɭamaɭamaɭa vaɭavaɭaə, takoʔiasə, "ʔiniɭaɭəðəkənai, ka posavoʔokai. maavaʔia pakono moa loŋai."

S.FORM: 'ipilikai ka mani poelrenai longalangai. "omikimo'o la mani dholromi'iae pasopalra." mani iiae omoli.
S.PHON: ʔipilikai ka mani poəɭənai loŋalaŋai. "omikimoʔo la mani ðoɭomiʔiaə pasopaɭa." mani iiaə omoli.

S.TRANSL: 我們的東西很快就賣完了，奶奶說：＂都是因為有你的幫忙。＂
Our goods sold out quickly, and my grandmother said, \It's all thanks to your help.\""

S.FORM: 'irakilrao 'ina tako'ica'oca'o mani lriho'ongalrao 'ina kaocae 'alredheedheke la akanekanae 'i lrihilae. omoangalrao 'itokalae 'ina'i talangalangalangai vare'aeni.
S.PHON: ʔirakiɭao ʔina takoʔiʦaʔoʦaʔo mani ɭihoʔoŋaɭao ʔina kaoʦaə ʔaɭəðəəðəkə la akanəkanaə ʔi ɭihilaə. omoaŋaɭao ʔitokalaə ʔinaʔi talaŋalaŋalaŋai varəʔaəni.

S.TRANSL: 這個活動，我學到了有關原住民植物和食品的知識，並且體驗到了擺攤的辛苦。
Through this event, I learned about indigenous plants and food and experienced the hard work of running a stall.

S.TRANSL: 看到了奶奶對傳統食物的熱愛，我感到非常驕傲和高興。
Seeing my grandmother's passion for traditional food, I feel very proud and honored.
